items.title: Enhanced Overspend Resolution, Mitigation and Procedure
items.description: Need to implement a cohesive policy and procedure workflow for overspending under the Support at Home program. When urgent action is required the current methods do not allow for a prompt response and this can leave clients without the care they require.
items.resolution: Prevention & Mitigation – system, training, and procedures. Determining Cause – informed choice by the client or failure in obligations. Resolution & Write-Offs – timeframes, escalation pathways. Care Continuity – community and support at home options. Governance & Continuous Improvement - complaints, reporting, training, and learning loops.

items.title: Cab Charge Optimisation & Risk Mitigation Initiative
items.description: Changes to Cab Charge card processes and system limitations (including tap-and-go functionality) introduced risks relating to card activation, usability at first use, and renewal requirements. These issues created potential delays at the point of service, impacting client experience and provider reliability.
items.resolution: Improved reliability and usability of Cab Charge cards at first use. Reduced likelihood of service disruption at the point of transport. Faster resolution of card-related issues. Strengthened internal controls.

items.title: Transport Service Continuity Enhancement (Alternative Provider Path)
items.description: A review identified that reliance on the largest transport provider (Cab Charge) created a risk of service disruption, particularly where card failures or delays in setup occur. While alternative options were available, they were not consistently structured or proactively implemented across all client types.
items.resolution: Created process for alternative provider and associated registration.

items.title: Client Agreement Review and Supporting Documents
items.description: Client complaints raised questions regarding Client Agreement clauses, including requests for removal or clarification, alongside recurring feedback themes regarding clarity and accessibility. Key Triggers: Client complaints regarding the Client Agreement (specific questions or requests to remove sections). Aim: improve clarity, accessibility, and practical understanding for clients. Review alongside recontracting for SAH lessons learnt.
items.resolution: Desired Outcomes: Practical alternative solution provided to client without requiring amendment of the core agreement. Improved tools for future clients (companion guide, targeted FAQ updates). Strengthened governance and oversight of client communications.

items.title: Communications (Quality Assurance Process)
items.description: Feedback received through recent reviews and provider interactions highlighted opportunities to strengthen communication practices within the Quality Assurance process. This included concerns around the tone and clarity of communication, timeliness of responses, and the level of contact maintained during and after incident investigations.
items.resolution: A revised incident management and investigation process has been developed, outlining clearer stages of communication and expected timeframes. Standardised templates are being introduced. Regular review of communication practices will occur as part of ongoing quality monitoring.

items.title: Complaints Framework
items.description: Review of recent complaint responses highlighted the need for a more structured, step-based approach to ensure consistent communication, stakeholder engagement, and governance oversight throughout the complaint management process. Trilogy Care is developing a refined Complaint Management Framework that embeds clear procedural steps, defined communication pathways, and accountability points.

items.title: Marketing Communication (Support at Home Program)
items.description: Feedback on communication to be sent to clients regarding upcoming changes with Support at Home Program.

items.title: Behavioural and Communication Plans
items.description: Trilogy Care has identified the need to strengthen individualised strategies for clients with cognitive impairment and/or behavioural challenges. Without tailored approaches, communication may not be fully effective or person-centred for clients with specific support needs. Implementing structured Behavioural and Communication Plans to guide staff engagement.
items.resolution: Trilogy Care will introduce Behavioural and Communication Support Plans for clients with diagnosed cognitive impairment incorporating tailored communication strategies, use of simplified language, visual aids, and support from carers or advocates. Staff training to identify communication barriers.

items.title: Provider Form for Terminating Services — Website Notifications
items.description: Invoices from providers on automation had not been stopped. Provider notified Coordinator however Trilogy notified 2 months later causing invoices paid incorrectly. Process will allow easier access to providers to complete termination to notify compliance who will follow up.

items.title: Management of Care Recipient Treatment Refusals
items.description: Develop a policy addressing cases where a care recipient requires treatment but refuses care. This was identified as part of the HRVC meetings.

items.title: Personal Accident Insurance Requirement Removal
items.description: Trilogy Care currently requires sole traders to hold Personal Accident Insurance. PA Insurance does not protect care recipients — it only provides income protection for the worker. The waiver creates an unnecessary barrier for care recipients in accessing care. There is no Aged Care Standard requiring PA Insurance, and risk is already managed through Public Liability Insurance.
items.resolution: Removed the mandatory requirement for PA Insurance. Eliminated the waiver process to reduce unnecessary steps for care recipients. Provide factual information to workers on PA Insurance without making it a requirement. Updated onboarding and policy documents.
